title: No Internet Connection
shortDescription: The TV cannot connect to the internet or the network connection has been lost.
fullDescription:
Error code 0-1 on Samsung Smart TVs indicates that the television has lost its internet connection or is unable to establish one. This is one of the most common Samsung TV errors and typically appears when trying to use streaming apps like Netflix, YouTube, or Samsung TV Plus.

The error can be caused by issues with your home Wi-Fi network, the TV's wireless adapter, DNS settings, or your internet service provider. It may also appear after a firmware update that resets network settings or when the router's DHCP lease expires.

In most cases, this error is resolved through basic network troubleshooting steps such as restarting the router and TV, or re-entering Wi-Fi credentials. Persistent issues may indicate a hardware problem with the TV's built-in Wi-Fi module.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi router is offline or out of range
TV Wi-Fi settings are incorrect or corrupted

symptoms: Error 0-1 displayed on screen when opening apps
Streaming apps fail to load content
Samsung TV Plus channels not available
Network status shows disconnected in TV settings
Smart Hub features unavailable

fixSteps.title: Restart the TV and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet and unplug your router. Wait 60 seconds. Plug the router back in first and wait for it to fully boot (all lights stable). Then plug the TV back in and turn it on.

fixSteps.title: Reconnect to Wi-Fi
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Open Network Settings > Wireless. Select your Wi-Fi network and re-enter the password. Wait for the connection test to complete.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > IP Settings. Change DNS Setting to 'Enter manually' and type 8.8.8.8 for the primary DNS and 8.8.4.4 for the secondary DNS (Google's public DNS servers).

fixSteps.title: Reset Smart Hub
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Reset Smart Hub. Enter your PIN (default is 0000). The TV will reset all Smart Hub data and re-download app configurations.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset network settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Reset Network Settings. This clears all saved network configurations. After reset, set up the Wi-Fi connection from scratch.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after all troubleshooting steps and the Wi-Fi module appears to have failed, a technician can replace the internal wireless adapter.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV error 0-1 no internet connection fix

faq.q: Why does my Samsung TV keep losing internet connection?
faq.a: Frequent disconnections usually point to Wi-Fi signal interference, router distance, or a weak Wi-Fi module. Try moving the router closer, using a 5GHz band, or connecting via Ethernet cable for a stable connection.

faq.q: Can I use an Ethernet cable instead of Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Yes. Connecting your Samsung TV directly to the router with an Ethernet cable provides a more stable connection and often resolves wireless-related error codes like 0-1.

faq.q: Does this error mean my TV is broken?
faq.a: Usually not. Error 0-1 is almost always a network configuration issue rather than a hardware defect. Only in rare cases is the Wi-Fi module itself faulty.

title: General Network Failure
shortDescription: A general network communication error has occurred preventing the TV from accessing online services.
fullDescription:
Error code 0-0 is a general network failure code on Samsung Smart TVs. Unlike the more specific 0-1 code, error 0-0 indicates a broader communication failure between the TV and Samsung's servers or streaming services.

This error commonly appears when Samsung's own servers are experiencing downtime, when there is a widespread internet outage in your area, or when the TV's software has encountered a network stack error. It can also occur when the TV's date and time settings are incorrect, which causes SSL certificate validation to fail.

The error is usually temporary and resolves on its own once server-side issues are fixed. However, if it persists, local troubleshooting such as resetting network settings or updating the TV firmware is recommended.

causes.cause: Incorrect date and time settings on the TV
Corrupted network stack in TV software

symptoms: Error 0-0 displayed when launching streaming apps
All Smart TV features are unavailable
Apps load but content fails to stream
Samsung TV Plus shows black screen with error
Smart Hub displays connection error

fixSteps.title: Check Samsung server status
fixSteps.instruction: Use a phone or computer to visit downdetector.com and search for Samsung TV. If many users are reporting issues, the problem is server-side and you need to wait for Samsung to resolve it.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV, unplug it from the wall for 30 seconds, then hold the power button on the TV itself (not the remote) for 5 seconds while unplugged. This drains residual power and forces a full reset. Plug back in and turn on.

fixSteps.title: Verify date and time settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock. Ensure the clock mode is set to Auto. If it shows the wrong date or time, set it manually to the correct values, then switch back to Auto.

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. If the TV has internet access, it will check for and install the latest firmware. If not, download the update from samsung.com onto a USB drive and use the USB update option.

fixSteps.title: Reset Smart Hub
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Reset Smart Hub. This resets all app data and may resolve corrupted network configurations. You will need to re-sign into your streaming apps afterward.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists for more than 24 hours and other devices on the same network work fine, contact Samsung support for remote diagnostics.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV error 0-0 fix streaming not working

faq.q: Is error 0-0 a sign my TV is defective?
faq.a: No. Error 0-0 is almost always caused by server-side issues or temporary network problems, not a hardware defect in the TV.

faq.q: How long do Samsung server outages usually last?
faq.a: Most Samsung server issues are resolved within a few hours. Major outages rarely last more than 24 hours.

faq.q: Why do I get this error on only one app?
faq.a: Some apps connect through different servers. If only one app shows error 0-0, that specific streaming service's connection to Samsung may be experiencing issues.

title: Smart Hub Connection Error
shortDescription: Smart Hub is unable to connect to Samsung servers to load apps and services.
fullDescription:
Error code 0001 on Samsung TVs indicates that the Smart Hub platform cannot establish a connection to Samsung's cloud servers. Smart Hub is the central interface for all apps, streaming services, and smart features on Samsung TVs.

This error typically occurs when the TV's network connection is unstable, when Samsung's Smart Hub servers are down, or after a software update that corrupted the Smart Hub data. It can also appear if the TV's region settings don't match the Samsung account region.

When this error occurs, you may still be able to use basic TV functions like watching broadcast channels and using HDMI inputs, but all smart features including apps, voice assistants, and Samsung TV Plus will be unavailable.

causes.cause: Smart Hub data corruption after failed update
Region mismatch between TV and Samsung account

symptoms: Smart Hub fails to load with error 0001
Apps appear as greyed-out icons
Samsung TV Plus is unavailable
Voice assistant features do not respond
App store cannot be accessed

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet for 60 seconds. While unplugged, press and hold the power button on the TV for 10 seconds to drain residual power. Plug back in and turn on.

fixSteps.title: Reset Smart Hub
fixSteps.instruction: Navigate to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Reset Smart Hub. Enter PIN 0000 when prompted. Wait for the reset to complete and the TV to reload Smart Hub.

fixSteps.title: Clear cache and data
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage. Select individual apps showing issues and choose 'Clear Cache' and 'Clear Data'. This removes corrupted temporary files.

fixSteps.title: Update TV software
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. Install any available updates. If the TV cannot connect to download updates, use a USB drive with the firmware downloaded from samsung.com/support.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: As a last resort, go to Settings > General > Reset. Enter PIN 0000. This restores the TV to factory settings and removes all customizations, accounts, and app data. You will need to set up the TV from scratch.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after a factory reset and firmware update, contact Samsung support as the issue may require a remote firmware flash or main board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV error code 0001 Smart Hub fix

faq.q: Will resetting Smart Hub delete my apps?
faq.a: Yes, resetting Smart Hub removes all installed apps and their data. You will need to re-download apps and sign back into each streaming service.

faq.q: Can I use my TV while Smart Hub is not working?
faq.a: Yes, basic functions like antenna/cable TV, HDMI inputs, and external device connections will still work. Only smart features and apps are affected.

faq.q: Why does error 0001 keep coming back?
faq.a: Recurring error 0001 often indicates a persistent network issue or corrupted firmware. Try a factory reset and ensure your router provides a stable connection to the TV.

title: App Download or Update Failure
shortDescription: The TV is unable to download or update an application from the Samsung App Store.
fullDescription:
Error code 0003 on Samsung Smart TVs indicates a failure during the download or update process of applications from the Samsung App Store. This error prevents apps from being installed or updated on the TV.

The most common cause is insufficient storage space on the TV's internal memory. Samsung TVs have limited storage for apps, and over time as apps grow in size, the storage can fill up. Other causes include network interruptions during download, Samsung server issues, or corrupted app data.

This error can affect both new app installations and automatic updates for existing apps. When it occurs, you may notice that some apps stop working or display outdated content because they require an update to function properly.

causes.cause: Insufficient storage space on the TV
TV firmware incompatible with app version

symptoms: Error 0003 when trying to download or update apps
App download progress bar stops and shows error
Apps fail to update automatically
App Store shows download error message
Recently updated apps crash on launch

fixSteps.title: Check and free storage space
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage. Review installed apps and delete any you no longer use. Clearing cache for large apps can also free space.

fixSteps.title: Clear App Store cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage. Find the Samsung App Store (or Apps), select it and choose Clear Cache. Restart the TV and try the download again.

fixSteps.title: Test network speed
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > Network Test. Ensure download speed is at least 10 Mbps for reliable app downloads. If speed is low, restart your router or move it closer to the TV.

fixSteps.title: Reset Smart Hub
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Reset Smart Hub. This clears all app data and reinstalls the default apps. Re-download the apps you need after the reset.

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. An outdated firmware version may be incompatible with newer app versions, causing download failures.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after clearing storage and resetting Smart Hub, and other devices can download from the internet without issue, contact Samsung support for remote diagnostics.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV error 0003 app download failed fix

faq.q: How much storage does a Samsung TV have for apps?
faq.a: Most Samsung Smart TVs have between 4GB and 8GB of internal storage for apps. System software uses a significant portion, leaving roughly 1-4GB available for user apps.

faq.q: Can I expand Samsung TV storage?
faq.a: Unlike smartphones, Samsung TVs do not support external storage for apps. You can only manage space by removing unused apps and clearing cache data.

faq.q: Why can't I download Netflix on my Samsung TV?
faq.a: If Netflix won't download, ensure your TV has enough storage space and a stable internet connection. On very old Samsung TV models (pre-2015), Netflix may no longer be supported.

title: Network Timeout Error
shortDescription: The TV's connection to the server timed out before a response was received.
fullDescription:
Error code 0-2 on Samsung TVs indicates a network timeout. The TV attempted to connect to a server but did not receive a response within the expected time frame. This is different from a complete disconnection (error 0-1) because the TV has an active network connection but cannot complete data transfers.

This timeout error commonly appears when the internet connection is too slow for streaming, when there is high network congestion, or when a firewall or router setting is blocking the TV's communication. It can also occur when Samsung's content delivery network (CDN) is experiencing high load.

The error is particularly common during peak usage hours when both your home network and streaming servers are under heavy load. It may also appear when using a VPN that routes traffic through a distant server.
symptoms: Error 0-2 when loading streaming content
Apps take a very long time to open then display error
Video playback buffers frequently before error appears
Smart Hub loads slowly and partially
Network test shows connected but slow speeds

fixSteps.title: Run a network speed test
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > Network Test. Check if the download speed is at least 25 Mbps for 4K content or 10 Mbps for HD content. If below, the connection is too slow.

fixSteps.title: Restart router and TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both the router and TV. Wait 60 seconds. Plug the router in first and wait for full boot-up. Then plug in the TV. This clears network caches and re-establishes a fresh connection.

fixSteps.title: Switch to a wired connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable from your router directly to the TV's LAN port. Go to Settings > General > Network > Open Network Settings > Wired. A wired connection eliminates Wi-Fi interference and provides consistent speeds.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS servers
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > IP Settings. Set DNS to manual and enter 1.1.1.1 (Cloudflare) or 8.8.8.8 (Google) as the primary DNS. This can speed up domain resolution times.

fixSteps.title: Disable VPN or proxy on router
fixSteps.instruction: If your router runs a VPN, temporarily disable it to test if it is causing the timeout. VPNs add latency that can trigger timeout errors. Access your router settings at 192.168.1.1 or similar.

whenToCallPro: If the error consistently occurs only on one streaming service while other apps work fine, the issue may be on the service provider's end. Contact Samsung support if all apps time out despite fast internet on other devices.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV error 0-2 timeout fix streaming

faq.q: Why does error 0-2 only happen in the evening?
faq.a: Network congestion peaks during evening hours when many users in your area are streaming. Your ISP may also throttle speeds during peak times. A wired connection and faster internet plan can help.

faq.q: Is error 0-2 the same as buffering?
faq.a: They are related. Buffering is a symptom of slow data transfer. Error 0-2 occurs when the data transfer is so slow that the TV gives up waiting and displays a timeout error.

faq.q: Should I upgrade my internet plan?
faq.a: If your speed test consistently shows less than 25 Mbps, upgrading may help, especially for 4K streaming. However, first ensure the issue isn't caused by Wi-Fi interference by testing with a wired connection.

title: Streaming Service Authentication Error
shortDescription: The TV cannot authenticate with a streaming service, typically related to expired or invalid session tokens.
fullDescription:
Error code 8003 on Samsung Smart TVs is an authentication failure that occurs when the TV's streaming app cannot verify your account credentials with the streaming service's servers. This error is frequently reported with apps like Netflix, Disney+, Amazon Prime Video, and other subscription services.

The error typically occurs when the authentication token stored by the app has expired, when the streaming service has changed its authentication protocol, or when there are too many simultaneous streams on the account. It can also appear after a password change on the streaming account that hasn't been updated on the TV.

In most cases, signing out of the affected app and signing back in resolves the issue by generating a fresh authentication token. If the error affects multiple apps, a Smart Hub reset may be necessary.

causes.cause: Expired authentication token in the app
Password changed on the streaming account
Too many simultaneous streams on the account

symptoms: Error 8003 displayed when opening a streaming app
Streaming app asks to sign in repeatedly
Content loads but playback fails with authentication error
Multiple streaming apps show sign-in errors simultaneously
Error appears after changing password on another device

fixSteps.title: Sign out and back into the app
fixSteps.instruction: Open the affected streaming app. Go to its Settings or Account section and select Sign Out. Wait 10 seconds, then sign back in with your current credentials.

fixSteps.title: Update the affected app
fixSteps.instruction: Go to the Samsung App Store. Find the affected app and check for updates. Install any available update and restart the app.

fixSteps.title: Clear app cache and data
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage. Select the affected app and choose Clear Cache, then Clear Data. Reopen the app and sign in again.

fixSteps.title: Check streaming account status
fixSteps.instruction: Using a phone or computer, log into the streaming service's website. Verify your subscription is active, your password is current, and check the number of active devices. Remove any unauthorized devices.

fixSteps.title: Re-sync Samsung account
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > System Manager > Samsung Account. Sign out of your Samsung account, restart the TV, then sign back in. This refreshes all account-linked app authorizations.

whenToCallPro: This error does not require professional repair. If you cannot resolve it by signing out and back in, contact the specific streaming service's customer support for account-related issues.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV error 8003 streaming authentication fix

faq.q: Why does error 8003 keep appearing on Netflix?
faq.a: Netflix periodically refreshes its security tokens. If your TV app has a stale token, signing out and back in forces a new token to be created, which usually fixes the issue permanently.

faq.q: Does error 8003 mean someone hacked my account?
faq.a: Not necessarily, but it can occur if someone else logged into your account and reached the maximum device limit. Check your account's active sessions on the streaming service's website.

faq.q: Why do all my streaming apps show this error at once?
faq.a: If multiple apps fail simultaneously, the issue is likely with your Samsung account sync or the TV's date/time settings rather than individual app problems. Try re-syncing your Samsung account.

title: General System Error
shortDescription: A general system error has occurred in the TV's operating system.
fullDescription:
ERROR-0 is a general system error on Samsung Smart TVs that indicates the Tizen operating system has encountered an unspecified fault. This catch-all error code can appear during various operations including app launches, system settings changes, or firmware updates.

The error is often caused by software corruption, insufficient system memory due to too many apps running, or a failed firmware update that left the system in an inconsistent state. It may also appear when the TV's internal storage is nearly full, preventing the system from creating temporary files needed for normal operation.

While ERROR-0 sounds alarming, it is usually resolvable through software troubleshooting. A power cycle or factory reset resolves the issue in the vast majority of cases. Hardware-related ERROR-0 occurrences are rare and typically accompanied by other symptoms like screen artifacts or audio distortion.
symptoms: ERROR-0 displayed on screen during various operations
TV freezes and becomes unresponsive
Apps crash immediately after launching
Settings menu fails to open or save changes
TV restarts unexpectedly

fixSteps.title: Cold boot the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet. Wait a full 2 minutes (this allows capacitors to discharge and volatile memory to clear). Plug back in and turn on. This is more thorough than a normal restart.

fixSteps.title: Free up system resources
fixSteps.instruction: Close all running apps by pressing the Home button, then navigating to recently opened apps and closing them one by one. Also go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage and delete unused apps.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware via USB
fixSteps.instruction: Visit samsung.com/support and download the latest firmware for your TV model to a USB drive. Insert the USB into the TV and go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. Select the USB option.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Reset. Enter PIN 0000. The TV will erase all data and restore to factory defaults. Set up the TV from scratch after the reset completes.

fixSteps.title: Service menu diagnostics
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after factory reset, the main board may need diagnostics. Contact Samsung support to request a service visit. Do not attempt to access the service menu without professional guidance.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after a factory reset and USB firmware update, the main board may be failing. A Samsung-certified technician should diagnose and replace the board if necessary.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV ERROR-0 general system error fix

faq.q: Is ERROR-0 a serious problem?
faq.a: Usually not. ERROR-0 is most often a software issue that can be resolved with a power cycle or factory reset. Only in rare cases does it indicate a hardware failure.

faq.q: Will factory reset fix ERROR-0?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. A factory reset clears all software corruption and restores the TV to a known working state. Ensure you have your Wi-Fi password and streaming account credentials ready before resetting.

faq.q: Can ERROR-0 damage my TV?
faq.a: No. ERROR-0 is a software-level error that does not cause hardware damage. However, if it's caused by a hardware issue, the underlying hardware problem should be addressed.

title: App Launch Failure
shortDescription: A specific application failed to launch on the Samsung Smart TV.
fullDescription:
ERROR-001 on Samsung TVs indicates that a specific application has failed to initialize and launch. This error is displayed when the Tizen operating system cannot load an app's executable code into memory, whether due to corrupted app data, insufficient resources, or incompatibility.

This error is different from network-related errors because it occurs at the app execution level rather than during network communication. The app's local data may be corrupted, the app version may be incompatible with the current firmware, or the system may not have enough available RAM to run the app.

The error most frequently occurs with resource-intensive apps like Netflix, YouTube, and gaming apps. It can also appear after a Tizen OS update if the app hasn't been updated to support the new OS version.

causes.cause: App version incompatible with TV firmware

symptoms: ERROR-001 displayed when trying to open a specific app
App icon appears on home screen but crashes immediately
Loading spinner shows briefly before error appears
Other apps work fine but one specific app fails
App worked previously but stopped after an update

fixSteps.title: Clear the app cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage. Select the failing app and choose Clear Cache. Try launching the app again.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall the app
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the app icon on the home screen and select Remove (or go to Manage Storage and delete it). Then open the Samsung App Store, find the app, and install it fresh.

fixSteps.title: Close background apps
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Home button and use the Recent Apps viewer to close all running apps. This frees up RAM for the app that is failing to launch.

fixSteps.title: Update the TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. App compatibility issues are often resolved by updating to the latest firmware version.

fixSteps.title: Reset Smart Hub
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Reset Smart Hub. Enter PIN 0000. This reinstalls all apps from scratch and can fix deep corruption issues.

whenToCallPro: If a specific app consistently fails to launch after reinstalling and resetting Smart Hub, the app developer may have dropped support for your TV model. Check the app's compatibility list or contact Samsung support.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV ERROR-001 app not opening fix

faq.q: Why does only one app show ERROR-001?
faq.a: Each app has its own data and installation files. If only one app is affected, its specific data is likely corrupted. Reinstalling that single app usually fixes the issue.

faq.q: Is my TV too old for this app?
faq.a: Samsung drops app support for older TV models over time. If your TV is more than 5 years old, check the app developer's website for a list of supported Samsung TV models.

faq.q: Can I sideload apps on Samsung TV?
faq.a: Samsung TVs run the Tizen OS which does not officially support sideloading. Apps must be installed through the Samsung App Store.

title: No Signal Detected on Input
shortDescription: The TV is not detecting any signal from the selected input source.
fullDescription:
The 'No Signal' message on Samsung TVs indicates that the television is not receiving any video signal from the currently selected input source (HDMI, component, antenna, etc.). This is one of the most common issues Samsung TV owners encounter.

The message appears when the TV is set to an input that has no active device connected, when the connected device is powered off or in standby, when the HDMI cable is faulty or loose, or when there is an HDMI handshake failure between the TV and the source device.

For antenna users, 'No Signal' can indicate a disconnected antenna cable, weak broadcast signal, or the need to rescan for channels. For HDMI users, it often points to cable issues, incorrect input selection, or HDMI port problems. Samsung TVs have an auto-detection feature (Anynet+/HDMI-CEC) that should switch inputs automatically, but this feature can sometimes malfunction.

causes.cause: Wrong input source selected on TV
Connected device is powered off or in standby

symptoms: 'No Signal' message on a blue, black, or grey screen
TV shows input name but no picture
Screen briefly flashes then shows No Signal
Sound works but no picture
Antenna channels missing or unavailable

fixSteps.title: Select the correct input
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Source button on your Samsung remote. Check which input your device is connected to (HDMI 1, HDMI 2, etc.) and select it. If using an antenna, select TV or Antenna.

fixSteps.title: Check cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug and firmly reconnect the HDMI cable (or antenna cable) at both ends — the TV port and the source device. Ensure the cable is fully seated and clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the source device
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the connected device (cable box, game console, streaming stick, etc.), unplug it for 30 seconds, then plug it back in and turn it on. Wait for it to fully boot before checking the TV.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the current HDMI cable with a known working cable. Damaged or low-quality cables are a common cause of No Signal errors, especially with 4K content. Use a certified Ultra High Speed HDMI cable for 4K TVs.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI port
fixSteps.instruction: Move the HDMI cable to a different HDMI port on the TV. If the signal works on another port, the original port may be damaged. Samsung TVs typically have 3-4 HDMI ports.

fixSteps.title: Rescan antenna channels
fixSteps.instruction: If using an antenna, go to Settings > Broadcasting > Auto Tuning (or Auto Program). Select Antenna or Cable and start the scan. This will find all available broadcast channels in your area.

whenToCallPro: If you've tried multiple cables and ports with no success, the HDMI port board or main board may need replacement. A technician can test the individual HDMI ports and replace the faulty board.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV no signal fix HDMI

faq.q: Why does my Samsung TV say No Signal when everything is connected?
faq.a: The most common reason is that the TV is set to the wrong input. Press the Source button and cycle through all inputs. Also ensure the connected device is actually powered on and not in standby mode.

faq.q: Can a bad HDMI cable cause No Signal?
faq.a: Yes. HDMI cables can develop internal breaks, especially at the connectors. If the cable is bent, kinked, or more than a few years old, replacing it is a quick and inexpensive fix.

faq.q: Why does No Signal appear after turning on my TV?
faq.a: The source device (cable box, console) may take longer to boot than the TV. Wait 30 seconds after turning everything on. You can also enable HDMI-CEC (Anynet+) so the TV automatically switches to the active input.

title: Unsupported Video Resolution or Refresh Rate
shortDescription: The incoming video signal has a resolution or refresh rate that the TV cannot display.
fullDescription:
The 'Mode Not Supported' message on Samsung TVs appears when the video signal coming from a connected device uses a resolution, refresh rate, or color format that the TV cannot process or display. Each Samsung TV model supports a specific set of video modes, and signals outside this range trigger this error.

This error is commonly seen when a PC or gaming console is set to output a resolution higher than the TV supports, when the refresh rate exceeds the TV's maximum (e.g., 120Hz signal to a 60Hz panel), or when the HDMI version doesn't match (e.g., HDMI 2.1 signal through an HDMI 2.0 port).

The issue can also occur when switching between devices that output different resolutions, when a Blu-ray player sends a 3D signal to a non-3D TV, or when the source device's HDR format is incompatible with the TV's HDR capabilities.

causes.cause: HDMI cable doesn't support required bandwidth
Incompatible HDR or color space format
Source device HDMI output version mismatch

symptoms: 'Mode Not Supported' message on screen
Screen goes black briefly then shows error
Picture appears for a moment then disappears
Screen flickers rapidly before showing error
Error appears only with certain devices or content

fixSteps.title: Change source device resolution
fixSteps.instruction: On your connected device (PC, console, cable box), go to display settings and lower the resolution. For most Samsung TVs, start with 1920x1080 at 60Hz. For 4K TVs, try 3840x2160 at 60Hz. Avoid custom or non-standard resolutions.

fixSteps.title: Lower the refresh rate
fixSteps.instruction: If using a gaming PC or console, change the refresh rate to 60Hz. Even if your TV supports 120Hz, the specific HDMI port you're using may only support 60Hz. HDMI 2.1 ports are required for 4K 120Hz.

fixSteps.title: Use the correct HDMI port
fixSteps.instruction: On Samsung TVs, only specific HDMI ports support the highest resolutions and refresh rates. Typically HDMI 4 (or the port labeled 'Game') supports HDMI 2.1 features like 4K 120Hz. Move your cable to this port.

fixSteps.title: Enable HDMI UHD Color / Input Signal Plus
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > External Device Manager > Input Signal Plus. Enable it for the HDMI port you're using. This unlocks 4K, HDR, and higher bandwidth support on that port.

fixSteps.title: Upgrade HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure you're using an Ultra High Speed HDMI cable (HDMI 2.1 certified) for 4K 120Hz or 8K signals. Older High Speed cables may not have enough bandwidth, causing the Mode Not Supported error.

whenToCallPro: This error rarely requires professional help. If the correct resolution is being sent and the error persists, the HDMI port or main board may have a hardware issue requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV mode not supported resolution fix

faq.q: What resolution does my Samsung TV support?
faq.a: Check your TV model specifications on samsung.com. Most modern Samsung TVs support up to 4K (3840x2160) at 60Hz. QLED and Neo QLED models with HDMI 2.1 ports also support 4K at 120Hz.

faq.q: Why does Mode Not Supported appear when I connect my PC?
faq.a: PCs can output many resolutions and refresh rates that TVs don't support. Set your PC to exactly 1920x1080 or 3840x2160 at 60Hz. Avoid ultrawide or non-standard resolutions.

faq.q: Can I use 4K 120Hz on my Samsung TV?
faq.a: Only Samsung TVs with HDMI 2.1 ports support 4K 120Hz. This includes QN90A and newer QLED/Neo QLED models. You must use the designated HDMI 2.1 port and an Ultra High Speed HDMI cable.

title: Netflix Network Connection Error
shortDescription: Netflix cannot connect to the Samsung TV's network, preventing streaming.
fullDescription:
Error code TVQ-ST-115 is a Netflix-specific error that appears on Samsung Smart TVs when the Netflix app cannot establish a network connection to Netflix's servers. While the TV may be connected to the internet and other apps may work, Netflix specifically cannot reach its streaming infrastructure.

This error can occur when the TV's DNS settings cannot resolve Netflix's server addresses, when a firewall or ISP is blocking Netflix traffic, or when the Netflix app's stored network configuration has become corrupted. It may also appear in regions where Netflix has recently launched or where ISPs throttle streaming traffic.

The error is usually indicated by the Netflix app displaying a message saying the device cannot reach the Netflix service along with the error code TVQ-ST-115 or the related code NW-2-5.

causes.cause: DNS resolution failure for Netflix servers
ISP blocking or throttling Netflix traffic

symptoms: Netflix displays error TVQ-ST-115
Netflix loading screen freezes then shows error
Other streaming apps work but Netflix does not
Netflix works on phone but not on the TV
Netflix home screen loads but content won't play

fixSteps.title: Restart the TV and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV and router for 60 seconds. Plug the router back in first, wait for full boot-up, then plug in the TV. Open Netflix and try again.

fixSteps.title: Sign out and back into Netflix
fixSteps.instruction: Open Netflix on the TV. Go to the Netflix menu (left sidebar) and scroll down to Get Help > Sign Out. Confirm sign-out. Then sign back in with your Netflix credentials.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > IP Settings. Change DNS to manual and enter 8.8.8.8 (Google DNS) or 1.1.1.1 (Cloudflare DNS). These public DNS servers reliably resolve Netflix addresses.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall Netflix
fixSteps.instruction: Go to the Samsung App Store, find Netflix, and select Reinstall (or delete and re-download). This replaces any corrupted app files with a fresh installation.

fixSteps.title: Check ISP and router settings
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your ISP is not blocking Netflix by testing Netflix on another device using the same network. If Netflix works on your phone but not the TV, check your router's firewall settings and ensure ports 80 and 443 are not blocked.

whenToCallPro: This is a software/network issue that does not require professional TV repair. If the issue persists, contact Netflix customer support directly as the problem may be account-related or on Netflix's end.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV Netflix error TVQ-ST-115 fix

faq.q: Why does Netflix not work but YouTube works fine?
faq.a: Each app connects to different servers. Netflix may be blocked by your DNS provider or router, while YouTube uses different network paths. Changing DNS settings to 8.8.8.8 often resolves this.

faq.q: Is Netflix dropping support for my Samsung TV?
faq.a: Netflix has dropped support for Samsung TVs manufactured before 2014. If your TV is from 2014 or earlier, Netflix may no longer be available as an app.

faq.q: Can I use a streaming stick if Netflix won't work on my TV?
faq.a: Yes. A Roku, Fire TV Stick, or Chromecast can run Netflix independently and connect to your Samsung TV via HDMI, bypassing any TV-specific Netflix issues.

title: YouTube App Playback Failure
shortDescription: The YouTube app on the Samsung TV fails to play videos or crashes during playback.
fullDescription:
The YouTube Playback Error on Samsung Smart TVs occurs when the YouTube app is unable to load or play video content. This can manifest as a black screen, buffering that never completes, an explicit error message, or the app crashing back to the home screen.

The YouTube app on Samsung TVs is maintained jointly by Google and Samsung, and issues can arise from outdated app versions, incompatible firmware, corrupted cache data, or network problems specific to YouTube's content delivery network. The error may also occur when YouTube changes its API and the TV app hasn't been updated to match.

This error is distinct from general network errors because it specifically affects YouTube while other apps may work fine. It can affect all YouTube content or only specific videos, particularly those in higher resolutions (4K, 8K) that require more bandwidth and processing power.

causes.cause: Insufficient bandwidth for selected video quality
Tizen OS compatibility issue with YouTube
YouTube API changes not yet supported

symptoms: YouTube videos show black screen with error
YouTube app crashes on launch
Videos buffer endlessly without playing
YouTube home screen loads but videos won't start
Audio plays but video is black

fixSteps.title: Clear YouTube app cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage. Select YouTube and choose Clear Cache. Restart the YouTube app.

fixSteps.title: Update the YouTube app
fixSteps.instruction: Open the Samsung App Store (Apps). Search for YouTube. If an update is available, select Update. Restart the app after updating.

fixSteps.title: Lower video quality settings
fixSteps.instruction: Open a YouTube video, tap the gear icon (Settings) during playback, and select Quality. Choose 1080p or 720p instead of Auto or 4K. This reduces bandwidth requirements.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall the YouTube app
fixSteps.instruction: Delete the YouTube app from your TV by holding the app icon and selecting Remove. Then re-download it from the Samsung App Store. Sign back into your YouTube/Google account.

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. Firmware updates often include fixes for app compatibility issues with YouTube and other Google services.

whenToCallPro: YouTube issues on Samsung TVs are software-related and do not require professional repair. If the problem persists, check Google's support page for known Samsung TV issues or use a streaming device as an alternative.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV YouTube not working playback error fix

faq.q: Why does YouTube crash on my Samsung TV?
faq.a: YouTube crashes are usually caused by an outdated app or corrupted cache data. Clearing the cache and updating the app resolves the issue in most cases.

faq.q: Will YouTube stop working on my Samsung TV?
faq.a: Google periodically drops YouTube support for older Samsung TV models. TVs from 2012 or earlier typically no longer receive YouTube updates. A streaming stick is the best alternative.

faq.q: Why do only 4K videos have problems?
faq.a: 4K videos require significantly more bandwidth and processing power. If your internet speed is below 25 Mbps or your TV model struggles with 4K decoding, lowering the quality to 1080p will help.

title: Wi-Fi Connection Keeps Dropping
shortDescription: The TV repeatedly loses its Wi-Fi connection requiring frequent reconnection.
fullDescription:
The 'WiFi Disconnected' issue on Samsung TVs is a persistent problem where the TV's wireless connection drops intermittently. Unlike a one-time connection failure, this issue involves repeated disconnections that can occur every few minutes to every few hours.

Samsung TVs have been known to have Wi-Fi connectivity issues related to the built-in wireless module, power management settings that put the Wi-Fi adapter to sleep, and compatibility issues with certain router configurations. The problem is particularly prevalent with dual-band routers where the TV switches between 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands.

The issue can be exacerbated by having many devices on the same network, using WPA3 encryption that some older Samsung TV models don't fully support, or router settings like band steering that force the TV between frequencies.

causes.cause: TV Wi-Fi power management putting adapter to sleep
Too many devices on the Wi-Fi network

symptoms: TV shows 'WiFi Disconnected' notification periodically
Streaming stops mid-playback with network error
Wi-Fi setting shows 'Not Connected' intermittently
TV reconnects to Wi-Fi on its own after a few minutes
Network apps sometimes work and sometimes don't

fixSteps.title: Assign a static IP to the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > IP Settings. Change IP Setting to 'Enter manually'. Enter an IP address outside your router's DHCP range (e.g., 192.168.1.200), the correct subnet mask (255.255.255.0), gateway (your router IP), and DNS (8.8.8.8).

fixSteps.title: Connect to a specific Wi-Fi band
fixSteps.instruction: If your router broadcasts separate 2.4GHz and 5GHz networks, connect the TV to the 5GHz network specifically. If the TV is far from the router, use 2.4GHz instead for better range. Avoid routers with band steering for the TV.

fixSteps.title: Change router encryption to WPA2
fixSteps.instruction: Access your router settings (typically at 192.168.1.1). Change the security mode from WPA3 to WPA2-PSK (AES). Some Samsung TVs have compatibility issues with WPA3 encryption.

fixSteps.title: Update TV firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Connect via Ethernet temporarily, then go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. Samsung has released multiple firmware updates that improve Wi-Fi stability on various models.

fixSteps.title: Use Ethernet connection
fixSteps.instruction: If Wi-Fi continues to be unreliable, connect the TV directly to the router with an Ethernet cable. This eliminates all wireless issues and provides the most stable connection for streaming.

whenToCallPro: If the Wi-Fi module itself is failing (disconnections happen with strong signal, multiple routers, and updated firmware), a technician can replace the internal Wi-Fi adapter board.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV WiFi keeps disconnecting fix

faq.q: Why does my Samsung TV keep losing Wi-Fi but other devices don't?
faq.a: Samsung TVs have known Wi-Fi compatibility issues with certain routers, especially those using WPA3 or aggressive band steering. The TV's Wi-Fi module is also less powerful than phones or laptops.

faq.q: Is the Samsung TV Wi-Fi module replaceable?
faq.a: Yes. The Wi-Fi module is a small board inside the TV that can be replaced by a technician. The part typically costs $15-40, and labor adds $100-200.

faq.q: Should I use 2.4GHz or 5GHz for my Samsung TV?
faq.a: Use 5GHz if the TV is within 15 feet of the router for faster speeds. Use 2.4GHz if the TV is farther away or there are walls between them, as 2.4GHz has better range and wall penetration.

title: Software/Firmware Update Failure
shortDescription: The TV failed to download or install a firmware update.
fullDescription:
The 'Software Update Failed' error on Samsung TVs occurs when the television cannot successfully download or install a firmware update. Samsung regularly releases firmware updates that include bug fixes, new features, security patches, and app compatibility improvements.

Update failures can happen during the download phase (due to network issues) or during the installation phase (due to power interruption, insufficient storage, or corrupted download files). A failed update can sometimes leave the TV in a partially updated state where some features may not work correctly.

This error is particularly concerning when it occurs during a critical security update or when apps require the new firmware version to function. In rare cases, a badly failed update can cause the TV to enter a boot loop or become unresponsive, requiring USB recovery.
symptoms: Update progress bar stops and shows failure message
TV restarts during update and shows error
Update downloads but fails to install
TV shows 'Update available' repeatedly but fails each time
TV stuck on Samsung logo after failed update

fixSteps.title: Retry the update with stable connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the TV to the router via Ethernet cable for maximum stability. Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now. Let the update complete without interrupting or turning off the TV.

fixSteps.title: Free up storage space
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Manage Storage. Delete unused apps and clear cache data to ensure enough space for the firmware update file.

fixSteps.title: Update via USB drive
fixSteps.instruction: Visit samsung.com/support. Enter your TV model number. Download the latest firmware file. Copy it to a USB drive (formatted as FAT32). Insert the USB into the TV and go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset then update
fixSteps.instruction: If the update keeps failing, perform a factory reset first: Settings > General > Reset (PIN: 0000). After the TV restarts and completes initial setup, immediately check for and install the update.

fixSteps.title: Recovery for boot loop
fixSteps.instruction: If the TV is stuck on the Samsung logo, unplug it for 5 minutes. Insert a USB drive with the firmware file. Plug the TV back in. Some models automatically detect the USB firmware and enter recovery mode. If not, contact Samsung support for guided recovery.

whenToCallPro: If the TV enters a boot loop after a failed update and USB recovery doesn't work, a Samsung technician can reflash the firmware using specialized service tools or replace the main board if it's corrupted.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV software update failed fix USB recovery

faq.q: Can a failed update brick my Samsung TV?
faq.a: It's rare but possible. If the TV loses power during the firmware write process, it can corrupt the system firmware. USB recovery resolves most cases, but severe corruption may require main board replacement.

faq.q: How do I find my Samsung TV model number?
faq.a: Press the Menu button on your remote and go to Settings > Support > About This TV. The model number is listed there. It's also on a sticker on the back of the TV.

faq.q: Should I enable auto-update on my Samsung TV?
faq.a: Auto-update is generally recommended as it keeps your TV secure and compatible with apps. However, if you've had update problems, you may want to disable it and update manually via USB for more control.

title: Screen Flickering or Flashing
shortDescription: The TV screen flickers, flashes, or blinks intermittently during use.
fullDescription:
Screen flickering on Samsung TVs is a visual disturbance where the display rapidly alternates between normal brightness and dimmer/brighter states, or between the picture and a black screen. This can range from subtle brightness fluctuations to severe flashing that makes the TV unwatchable.

The causes of flickering can be software-related (such as energy-saving settings, ambient light detection, or motion smoothing features) or hardware-related (such as a failing LED backlight, loose T-CON board connections, or a degrading power supply). Software causes are much more common and easier to resolve.

Samsung TVs with Eco Sensor (ambient light detection) are particularly prone to perceived flickering because the TV constantly adjusts brightness based on room lighting. Motion smoothing (Motion Plus/Auto Motion Plus) can also create a flickering effect, especially during fast-paced content like sports.

causes.cause: Eco Sensor / Ambient Light Detection adjusting brightness
Motion smoothing (Auto Motion Plus) causing visual artifacts
Energy Saving mode causing brightness fluctuations
Loose or damaged cable connection (HDMI, power)
T-CON board or power supply board failure

symptoms: Screen brightness fluctuates rapidly
Screen goes black for a split second then returns
Visible flashing or strobing effect
Flickering more noticeable in dark scenes
Flickering occurs only on certain inputs or apps

fixSteps.title: Disable Eco Sensor and Energy Saving
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Eco Solution (or Power and Energy Saving). Turn off Ambient Light Detection (Eco Sensor), Brightness Optimization, and Energy Saving Mode. Set the brightness manually.

fixSteps.title: Turn off Motion Smoothing
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > Expert Settings > Auto Motion Plus Settings. Set Auto Motion Plus to Off. This disables frame interpolation that can cause flickering artifacts.

fixSteps.title: Check all cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug and firmly reconnect the power cable and all HDMI cables. A loose power connection is a common cause of flickering. If using a power strip, plug the TV directly into a wall outlet to rule out power strip issues.

fixSteps.title: Disable Game Mode and VRR
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > External Device Manager. Turn off Game Mode and FreeSync/VRR (Variable Refresh Rate). These features can cause flickering with some content and devices.

fixSteps.title: Perform a picture test
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Picture Test. This displays a test image. If the test image flickers, the issue is with the TV hardware. If it doesn't flicker, the issue is with the content or source device.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: If software settings don't resolve the flickering, perform a factory reset: Settings > General > Reset (PIN: 0000). This eliminates any software configuration causing the issue.

whenToCallPro: If the flickering persists after disabling all software features and performing a factory reset, and the picture test shows flickering on the test image, the backlight, T-CON board, or power supply may be failing. These are internal hardware components that require professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV screen flickering fix settings

faq.q: Why does my Samsung TV flicker only during dark scenes?
faq.a: Samsung TVs use local dimming to darken parts of the screen for better contrast. Aggressive local dimming settings can cause visible flickering in dark scenes. Try setting Local Dimming to Low or Off in Picture > Expert Settings.

faq.q: Can a bad HDMI cable cause screen flickering?
faq.a: Yes. A damaged or low-quality HDMI cable can cause flickering, especially at 4K resolution. Replace the cable with a certified Ultra High Speed HDMI cable to rule this out.

faq.q: Is screen flickering covered under Samsung warranty?
faq.a: If the flickering is caused by a hardware defect (backlight or board failure) and the TV is under warranty, Samsung will repair or replace it at no cost. Software-related flickering is not considered a defect.

title: No Audio Output from TV
shortDescription: The TV produces no sound through its internal speakers or connected audio devices.
fullDescription:
The 'No Sound' issue on Samsung TVs is when the television displays video content normally but produces no audio from the internal speakers or connected external audio devices. This is a common problem that can affect the TV's built-in speakers, HDMI ARC/eARC connections to soundbars, optical audio output, or Bluetooth audio connections.

The issue can be caused by incorrect audio output settings (where the TV is set to output audio through a device that is not connected), muted or very low volume levels, incorrect audio format settings, or hardware issues with the TV's internal amplifier or speakers.

Samsung TVs have multiple audio output options and the settings can be confusing. The TV may be set to output through HDMI ARC when no soundbar is connected, or the audio format may be set to a format (like Dolby Digital+) that the connected audio device doesn't support.

causes.cause: TV audio output set to external device that's not connected
TV is muted or volume is at minimum
HDMI ARC/eARC handshake failure with soundbar

symptoms: Picture works but no sound from speakers
Volume controls respond but no audio plays
Sound works on some inputs but not others
Audio comes through TV speakers but not soundbar (or vice versa)
Sound cuts out intermittently during playback

fixSteps.title: Check mute and volume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Mute button on the remote to ensure the TV is not muted. Increase the volume to at least 20. Check that the volume indicator appears on screen when pressing volume buttons.

fixSteps.title: Set correct audio output
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Output. Select 'TV Speaker' if using internal speakers, or 'HDMI-ARC' / 'Optical' / 'Bluetooth' if using an external audio device. Ensure the selected device is connected and powered on.

fixSteps.title: Reset sound settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Expert Settings > Reset Sound. This restores all audio settings to factory defaults without affecting other TV settings.

fixSteps.title: Change audio format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Expert Settings > Digital Output Audio Format. Change from 'Auto' or 'Pass-through' to 'PCM'. PCM is universally compatible with all audio devices and eliminates format incompatibility issues.

fixSteps.title: Run a sound test
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Sound Test. The TV plays a test melody through the internal speakers. If you hear the melody, the speakers work and the issue is with settings or the source. If no sound, the speakers may be damaged.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle HDMI ARC connection
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both the TV and the soundbar/receiver. Wait 30 seconds. Plug in the TV first, then the soundbar. Ensure HDMI-CEC (Anynet+) is enabled on both devices. Test audio output.

whenToCallPro: If the sound test produces no audio through the internal speakers and all settings are correct, the TV's internal speakers or amplifier circuit may be damaged. A technician can diagnose and replace the speaker assembly or main board audio circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV no sound fix audio settings

faq.q: Why does my Samsung TV have no sound on Netflix but other apps work?
faq.a: Netflix may be outputting audio in a format your TV or soundbar doesn't support (like Dolby Atmos). Go to Netflix settings on the TV and change the audio to 'Stereo' or change the TV's Digital Output Audio Format to PCM.

faq.q: Why did my soundbar stop working with my Samsung TV?
faq.a: HDMI ARC connections can lose their handshake. Power cycle both devices, ensure the HDMI cable is in the ARC-labeled port on both devices, and enable HDMI-CEC (Anynet+) on both the TV and soundbar.

faq.q: Can I use Bluetooth headphones with my Samsung TV?
faq.a: Yes, most Samsung Smart TVs from 2017 onward support Bluetooth audio output. Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Output > Bluetooth Speaker List to pair your headphones.

title: HDMI ARC/eARC Audio Connection Failure
shortDescription: The HDMI ARC or eARC connection between the TV and soundbar/receiver is not working properly.
fullDescription:
HDMI ARC (Audio Return Channel) and eARC (Enhanced Audio Return Channel) errors on Samsung TVs occur when the audio connection between the TV and an external sound system (soundbar or AV receiver) fails. ARC/eARC allows the TV to send audio back to a soundbar through the same HDMI cable used for video, eliminating the need for a separate optical cable.

These errors can manifest as no audio from the soundbar, intermittent audio dropouts, audio delay (lip sync issues), or the TV and soundbar failing to recognize each other. The HDMI-CEC protocol (Samsung calls it Anynet+) is integral to ARC functionality, and problems with CEC often cause ARC failures.

Common causes include HDMI cables that don't support ARC, incorrect port usage (only one HDMI port on each device supports ARC), disabled CEC/Anynet+ settings, or firmware incompatibilities between the TV and soundbar. Samsung TVs with eARC support require Ultra High Speed HDMI cables for full functionality.

causes.cause: Cable connected to non-ARC HDMI port
Firmware incompatibility between TV and soundbar

symptoms: No audio from soundbar despite HDMI connection
Audio cuts in and out randomly
TV remote doesn't control soundbar volume
Audio is delayed (out of sync with video)
Soundbar shows disconnected from TV
Anynet+ doesn't detect the soundbar

fixSteps.title: Verify ARC port connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the HDMI cable is connected to the HDMI port labeled 'ARC' or 'eARC' on both the TV and the soundbar. On most Samsung TVs, this is HDMI 3 or the port specifically labeled. Check your TV manual for the exact port.

fixSteps.title: Enable Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC)
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > External Device Manager > Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC). Turn it On. This is required for ARC audio to work. Also enable it on the soundbar.

fixSteps.title: Set audio output to HDMI-ARC
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Sound Output and select 'HDMI-ARC' (or Receiver). If using eARC, also go to Settings > Sound > Expert Settings > HDMI eARC Mode and set it to Auto.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both the TV and the soundbar from power. Wait 60 seconds. Plug in the TV first, wait for it to turn on, then plug in the soundbar. This forces a fresh HDMI handshake.

fixSteps.title: Replace HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Use a High Speed HDMI cable (or Ultra High Speed for eARC). Not all HDMI cables support ARC. Try a new, certified cable to rule out cable issues.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware on both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Update the TV firmware (Settings > Support > Software Update) and check the soundbar manufacturer's website for firmware updates. Incompatibilities are often resolved through firmware updates.

partsNeeded.part: High Speed HDMI cable with ARC support
Ultra High Speed HDMI cable (for eARC)

whenToCallPro: If the ARC port on the TV has a hardware failure (no audio from ARC with multiple tested soundbars and cables), the HDMI port board may need replacement by a technician.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV HDMI ARC not working soundbar fix

faq.q: What's the difference between ARC and eARC?
faq.a: ARC supports basic audio formats like stereo and Dolby Digital 5.1. eARC supports higher-quality formats including Dolby Atmos and DTS:X. eARC requires an Ultra High Speed HDMI cable and both devices must support eARC.

faq.q: Can I use an optical cable instead of HDMI ARC?
faq.a: Yes. If ARC is causing problems, an optical (TOSLINK) cable is a reliable alternative. It supports stereo and Dolby Digital 5.1 but not Dolby Atmos. Change the TV's Sound Output to Optical.

faq.q: Why does my soundbar volume change on its own?
faq.a: With Anynet+ enabled, the TV tries to sync volume control with the soundbar. If this causes erratic behavior, you can try adjusting the Anynet+ settings or controlling the soundbar volume independently.

title: Samsung TV Plus Streaming Error
shortDescription: Samsung TV Plus free streaming service is unable to load or play channels.
fullDescription:
ERROR-012 on Samsung TVs specifically relates to the Samsung TV Plus service, which is a free ad-supported streaming television (FAST) service built into Samsung Smart TVs. This error indicates that the TV Plus app cannot load its channel guide or play the selected channel.

Samsung TV Plus requires an active internet connection and communicates with Samsung's content delivery servers to stream live channels. When the connection fails or the server is unavailable, error 012 is displayed. This error can also occur when Samsung TV Plus is not available in your country or region.

The error is different from general app errors because Samsung TV Plus is deeply integrated into the Tizen OS and cannot be uninstalled or reinstalled like other apps. It can only be reset through Smart Hub or system-level resets.

causes.cause: Internet connection too slow for streaming
TV Plus not available in your region

symptoms: ERROR-012 when selecting Samsung TV Plus channels
TV Plus channel guide shows but channels won't play
Loading spinner on TV Plus channels that never resolves
TV Plus tab missing from the home screen
Some TV Plus channels work but others show error

fixSteps.title: Check internet connection speed
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > Network Test. Samsung TV Plus requires at least 5 Mbps for SD content and 15 Mbps for HD content. If speed is below this, restart your router.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds, then plug it back in. This clears the TV Plus cache and forces a fresh connection to Samsung's servers.

fixSteps.title: Reset Smart Hub
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Device Care > Self Diagnosis > Reset Smart Hub. Enter PIN 0000. Since TV Plus is part of Smart Hub, this reset refreshes its configuration.

fixSteps.title: Check Samsung TV Plus server status
fixSteps.instruction: Use a phone or computer to search 'Samsung TV Plus down' on downdetector.com. If there's a widespread outage, you'll need to wait for Samsung to resolve it.

fixSteps.title: Verify region settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > System Manager > Language. Ensure the TV language and region match your actual location. Samsung TV Plus availability varies by country. Incorrect region settings can prevent access.

whenToCallPro: Samsung TV Plus issues are server-side or software-related and do not require professional repair. Contact Samsung support if the error persists after trying all steps.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV Plus error 012 not working fix

faq.q: Is Samsung TV Plus free?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung TV Plus is a free ad-supported streaming service available on Samsung Smart TVs from 2016 onward. It does not require a subscription or Samsung account.

faq.q: Can I disable Samsung TV Plus?
faq.a: You cannot fully uninstall Samsung TV Plus, but you can disable it from the home screen. Go to the TV Plus app, press and hold it, and select Remove. It will no longer appear prominently on the home screen.

faq.q: Is Samsung TV Plus available in all countries?
faq.a: No. Samsung TV Plus is available in select countries including the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and several European countries. If your TV's region is set to an unsupported country, TV Plus will not work.

title: Black Screen with No Picture
shortDescription: The TV powers on with sound and indicator lights but the screen remains completely black.
fullDescription:
The Black Screen issue on Samsung TVs, sometimes called the 'Black Screen of Death,' occurs when the TV appears to be on (power indicator lit, sound may be audible, remote responsive) but the screen shows no picture at all. This is distinct from a No Signal error because the TV is functioning but the display itself is not rendering an image.

This problem can be caused by software glitches (most common), backlight failure, a faulty T-CON board (timing controller), loose internal cable connections, or a failing main board. Software-related black screens usually respond to a power cycle, while hardware failures require physical repair.

A simple test to distinguish between software and hardware issues: shine a flashlight at the TV screen in a dark room while the TV is on. If you can faintly see a picture, the backlight has failed but the panel is working. If there's absolutely no picture even with a flashlight, the T-CON board or panel may be at fault.
symptoms: Screen is completely black but TV is powered on
Sound plays but no picture visible
Power LED indicator is lit
Remote control clicks are audible (channel changes, menu sounds)
TV briefly shows Samsung logo on startup then goes black

fixSteps.title: Perform a hard power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet. Wait 2 full minutes. While the TV is unplugged, press and hold the power button on the TV itself (not the remote) for 30 seconds. Plug back in and turn on.

fixSteps.title: Try a different input source
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Source button on the remote and switch to a different input (e.g., switch from HDMI1 to TV or HDMI2). If the picture appears on another input, the original source device or cable may be the issue.

fixSteps.title: Flashlight test for backlight failure
fixSteps.instruction: In a dark room with the TV on, hold a flashlight directly against the TV screen. Look very closely for a faint image. If you can see a dim picture, the LED backlight has failed and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check cable connections inside the TV
fixSteps.instruction: WARNING: Only attempt if comfortable working with electronics. Unplug the TV and remove the back panel screws. Check that the ribbon cables connecting the main board to the T-CON board and the T-CON board to the panel are firmly seated. Reseat any loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Contact Samsung or a repair technician
fixSteps.instruction: If the flashlight test reveals a backlight failure or reseating cables doesn't help, the TV needs professional repair. Contact Samsung support (1-800-726-7864) or a local TV repair shop. Have your model number and serial number ready.

whenToCallPro: If the flashlight test confirms a backlight failure, or if a power reset doesn't restore the picture, professional repair is needed. Backlight replacement requires disassembling the TV panel and is not recommended for DIY repair.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV black screen fix backlight test

faq.q: Is the Samsung black screen of death fixable?
faq.a: Yes, in most cases. Software-related black screens are fixed with a power reset. Hardware-related black screens (backlight or board failure) can be repaired by replacing the faulty component, typically costing $150-400 with labor.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix a Samsung TV backlight?
faq.a: LED backlight strips cost $30-150 depending on TV size. Professional labor adds $100-200. Total repair cost is typically $200-400. For TVs over 5 years old or budget models, replacement may be more cost-effective.

faq.q: Why does my TV show the Samsung logo then go black?
faq.a: This usually indicates a software issue where the TV boots successfully but then crashes. A factory reset via USB recovery or service menu may resolve it. If not, the main board may need replacement.

title: HDCP Content Protection Error
shortDescription: The TV cannot display protected content due to an HDCP (High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection) handshake failure.
fullDescription:
The HDCP error on Samsung TVs occurs when the High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection handshake between the TV and a connected source device fails. HDCP is a copy-protection technology that encrypts the signal between devices (Blu-ray players, streaming sticks, cable boxes, gaming consoles) and the TV to prevent unauthorized copying of copyrighted content.

When the HDCP handshake fails, the TV either shows a blank/purple screen, displays an HDCP error message, or shows a low-resolution version of the content. This is particularly common with 4K content that requires HDCP 2.2, while older content may use HDCP 1.4.

The error often occurs when using HDMI splitters or switches that don't support HDCP, when the HDMI cable doesn't properly support the required HDCP version, or when devices in the HDMI chain (like AV receivers) create HDCP compatibility issues. It can also happen when the TV or source device firmware needs updating.

causes.cause: HDMI cable not supporting HDCP 2.2 for 4K content
HDMI splitter or switch breaking HDCP chain
Source device or TV firmware bug

symptoms: HDCP error message on screen
Purple or pink screen when playing protected content
Content plays at reduced resolution (480p instead of 4K)
Streaming apps show 'content not available' error
Blu-ray player shows black screen for protected discs

fixSteps.title: Reseat HDMI connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug and firmly reconnect the HDMI cable at both ends. Turn off both the TV and source device, then turn the TV on first, followed by the source device. This forces a fresh HDCP handshake.

fixSteps.title: Remove HDMI splitters or switches
fixSteps.instruction: If using any HDMI splitter, switch, or adapters, remove them and connect the source device directly to the TV. Many splitters break the HDCP chain.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Use a Premium High Speed or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable. The cable must support HDCP 2.2 for 4K protected content. Look for cables labeled 'Premium Certified' or 'Ultra High Speed.'

fixSteps.title: Enable Input Signal Plus
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > General > External Device Manager > Input Signal Plus. Enable it for the HDMI port being used. This enables full HDCP 2.2 support on that port.

fixSteps.title: Update all devices in the HDMI chain
fixSteps.instruction: Update the firmware on the TV (Settings > Support > Software Update), the source device, and any AV receivers in the chain. HDCP compatibility issues are frequently fixed through firmware updates.

whenToCallPro: HDCP issues are almost always resolved by changing cables, removing splitters, or updating firmware. Professional help is only needed if you suspect the HDMI port's HDCP chip itself is faulty, which is very rare.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV HDCP error fix 4K content protection

faq.q: What is HDCP and why does my TV need it?
faq.a: HDCP (High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection) is an anti-piracy technology required by content providers. Your TV must support it to display protected content from streaming services, Blu-ray players, and cable boxes.

faq.q: Do I need HDCP 2.2 for all content?
faq.a: HDCP 2.2 is required for 4K protected content. Older HD content uses HDCP 1.4. If your setup only supports HDCP 1.4, 4K content may be downscaled to 1080p.

faq.q: Can an HDMI splitter cause HDCP errors?
faq.a: Yes. Most cheap HDMI splitters and switches do not properly pass through HDCP authentication. Either remove the splitter or invest in an HDCP 2.2 certified HDMI switch.

title: TV Overheating Warning or Shutdown
shortDescription: The TV displays a temperature warning or shuts down automatically due to excessive internal heat.
fullDescription:
The overheating warning on Samsung TVs appears when the internal temperature sensors detect that the TV has exceeded its safe operating temperature. This can result in a warning message on screen, automatic dimming of the backlight, or a complete shutdown to protect the internal components.

Samsung TVs generate heat through the LED backlight (or OLED panel), the main processor, and the power supply. Under normal conditions, passive cooling through ventilation slots is sufficient. However, when airflow is blocked, ambient temperature is very high, or internal components are failing, the TV can overheat.

Overheating is more common with TVs installed in enclosed spaces like cabinets, recessed wall mounts with no ventilation, or near heat sources like radiators and direct sunlight. It can also occur when the TV is running high-performance features like Game Mode with 4K 120Hz for extended periods.

causes.cause: Dust buildup blocking internal air circulation
Direct sunlight or heat source near TV
Failing internal cooling fan (if equipped) or power supply overheating

symptoms: Overheating warning message on screen
TV shuts off automatically during use
TV feels very hot to the touch on the back panel
Screen dims automatically despite brightness settings
TV turns off after extended gaming sessions

fixSteps.title: Ensure proper ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Make sure there is at least 4 inches (10 cm) of space on all sides and above the TV. If the TV is in a cabinet, open the cabinet doors or remove the TV. Do not place objects on top of the TV or cover the ventilation slots.

fixSteps.title: Clean dust from ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the TV. Use compressed air to gently blow out dust from the ventilation slots on the back and bottom of the TV. Do not use a vacuum cleaner directly on the vents.

fixSteps.title: Move away from heat sources
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the TV is not near a radiator, heating vent, fireplace, or in direct sunlight. Relocate the TV or use blackout curtains to reduce heat exposure from windows.

fixSteps.title: Reduce TV brightness and features
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture and reduce the Backlight/Brightness setting. Disable features that increase processor load: Game Mode, Auto Motion Plus, and local dimming. Lower resolution gaming (from 4K 120Hz to 4K 60Hz) also reduces heat.

fixSteps.title: Let the TV cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV and unplug it. Wait at least 30 minutes for it to cool completely. If the TV was in an enclosed space, relocate it to an open area before turning it back on.

partsNeeded.part: Replacement power supply board (if overheating internally)

whenToCallPro: If the TV overheats even with proper ventilation and moderate brightness, an internal component (power supply or main board) may be generating excessive heat. A technician should inspect and replace the faulty component.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung TV overheating shutdown fix ventilation

faq.q: Is it normal for a Samsung TV to feel warm?
faq.a: Yes. The back panel of a Samsung TV will feel warm during normal operation. However, it should never be too hot to touch. If it's painfully hot, there's an issue that needs addressing.

faq.q: Can overheating damage my Samsung TV?
faq.a: Prolonged overheating can degrade internal components and shorten the TV's lifespan. The automatic shutdown feature protects against immediate damage, but chronic overheating should be resolved.

faq.q: Is the Samsung Frame TV more prone to overheating?
faq.a: The Frame TV can run warmer than regular TVs because it displays art images continuously in Art Mode. Ensure it is wall-mounted with adequate ventilation behind it, especially if Art Mode is used frequently.

title: TV Remote Control Not Responding
shortDescription: The Samsung TV remote control is not responding or intermittently failing to control the TV.
fullDescription:
The 'Remote Not Working' issue on Samsung TVs occurs when the Samsung Smart Remote (or standard IR remote) stops responding to button presses. This can affect all buttons or only specific functions. Samsung Smart Remotes use both infrared (IR) and Bluetooth connections, and either can fail independently.

The Samsung Smart Remote connects to the TV via Bluetooth for voice commands, cursor navigation, and smart features, while using IR for basic functions like power and volume. If the Bluetooth pairing is lost, smart features won't work but basic IR functions may still operate. If both IR and Bluetooth fail, the remote is likely out of battery or has a hardware defect.

This issue can also be caused by interference from other IR devices, physical obstructions between the remote and TV's IR sensor, or a stuck button on the remote that's sending continuous signals.

causes.cause: Dead or weak batteries in the remote
Bluetooth pairing lost between remote and TV
TV IR sensor or Bluetooth module failure

symptoms: No buttons on the remote produce any response
Voice commands don't work but buttons do
Remote works only at close range
Some buttons work but others don't
Remote works intermittently

fixSteps.title: Replace the batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Open the battery compartment on the Samsung Smart Remote (slide the back panel down) and replace both AA batteries with fresh ones. For the solar-powered Smart Remote, charge it via USB-C or by placing it in sunlight.

fixSteps.title: Re-pair the Bluetooth connection
fixSteps.instruction: Point the remote at the TV from within 1 foot. Press and hold the Return button and the Play/Pause button simultaneously for 3 seconds. The TV should display a pairing message. Follow the on-screen instructions.

fixSteps.title: Reset the remote
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the batteries from the remote. Press and hold the Power button on the remote for 8 seconds (this discharges residual power). Reinsert the batteries and try again.

fixSteps.title: Clear obstructions from IR sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The TV's IR sensor is typically at the bottom center of the screen bezel. Ensure no objects (soundbar, decorations) are blocking the sensor. Clean the sensor area with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Use the SmartThings app as temporary remote
fixSteps.instruction: Download the Samsung SmartThings app on your smartphone. Connect to the same Wi-Fi network as the TV. The app can function as a full remote control while you troubleshoot or replace the physical remote.

whenToCallPro: Remote control issues do not require professional TV repair. If the remote itself is defective, purchase a replacement Samsung Smart Remote compatible with your TV model.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Smart Remote not working fix pairing

faq.q: Where can I buy a replacement Samsung remote?
